text.title: AI Code Compliance Validation Core Rules
text.summary: Five testable rules the senior dev MUST enforce on every AI-generated diff before a client PR opens.

rule.statement: The client's coding-standard charter is the authoritative input to the pass. The senior dev MUST NOT silently extend or relax the charter based on the AI's preferred style. Charter updates require a written change request to the client and an explicit charter version bump.

rationale.source: Outsource governance practice; SOC 2 change-management criterion

rule.rationale: Silent charter drift is the leading cause of "the previous senior dev let it slide" disputes. The charter is a contract artefact, not a wiki page; modifications need a paper trail.

rule.statement: The validation pass MUST run only over the AI-touched files in the current branch's diff. Running the lint against the entire repo wastes review time and produces noise unrelated to the PR. The `scripts/scope-diff.sh` output is the authoritative file list.

rationale.source: Practical lint adoption; the `lint-staged-only-not-whole-tree` sibling methodology

rule.rationale: Whole-tree lint output drowns the AI-specific findings in pre-existing noise. The senior dev's job is reviewing the AI's contribution; whole-tree review is a separate engagement.

rule.statement: Lint MUST run with the CLIENT's lint configuration (ruff.toml, eslint.config.mjs, .swiftlint.yml committed in the client repo) — NEVER the senior dev's personal lint profile or the AI tool's default. If the client config is absent, the pass HALTS until a config is obtained or generated via `client-conventions-reverse-engineering`.
rule.rationale: "It passed my lint" without the client lint is a recurring source of PR back-and-forth. Forcing client config eliminates the most common preventable rework.

rule.statement: The forbidden-APIs file (`forbidden-apis.yaml`) is INDEXED by compliance posture: SOC 2, HIPAA, PCI, GDPR, internal-only. The scan applies the union of postures the client declares. Common posture-bound rules: no `print()` / `console.log` to STDOUT in HIPAA; no unbounded `requests.get` without timeout; no `eval` / `exec`; no insecure crypto primitives.

rationale.source: Compliance audit findings literature; OWASP ASVS controls

rule.rationale: AI assistants emit logging and HTTP code by default that violates regulated postures. A posture-indexed forbidden-API scan is the cheapest defence against silently regressing the client's audit position.

rule.statement: Every finding in the validation report MUST be turned into a PR comment with: (a) the finding type; (b) the rule it violates; (c) the recommended fix; (d) an explicit `block` or `advise` severity tag. The senior dev signs off the report before any AI-generated diff lands in the client PR.
rule.rationale: Findings without rationale are dismissed by reviewers; findings without recommended fixes generate back-and-forth. The (a)-(d) structure is the minimum a client reviewer needs to act.

example.title: HIPAA posture catches a console.log
example.code: File: src/clinical/record-service.ts (AI-touched) Finding: console.log(patientRecord) at line 142 Rule: HIPAA Security Rule §164.312(a)(1) audit controls + forbidden-apis.yaml: posture=HIPAA / no_console_log_PHI Fix: remove the console.log; emit only sanitized audit-trail event via the audit-logger module already in the codebase. Severity: block
example.note: r4 + r5 satisfied. PR comment is unambiguous; senior dev does not need to research; AI fix is one-line.

example.title: Whole-tree lint noise
example.code: Validation report: 1,847 findings. File touched by AI: 4 files. Findings in AI-touched files: 12. Findings in other (pre-existing) files: 1,835. Recommendation: review all 1,847 before opening PR.
example.note: r2 violation. The senior dev's actual scope is 12 findings. Whole-tree review burns 2 days; AI-scope review takes 20 minutes.

example.title: Senior dev relaxes the charter silently
example.code: Charter: snake_case for Python identifiers. AI diff: introduces 14 camelCase identifiers. Senior dev action: opens PR anyway with comment "modern style".
example.note: r1 violation. The senior dev cannot relax the charter without a written client request. Client review will bounce the PR and erode trust.
